sections.title: Derivatives
sections.content: A derivative measures the instantaneous rate of change of a function. The chain rule differentiates a composite function by multiplying the outer derivative by the inner derivative.

formulas.description: Chain rule for differentiating composite functions.

sections.title: Matrices
sections.content: A matrix organizes numbers into rows and columns. Matrix multiplication combines rows and columns and is defined only when the inner dimensions match.

formulas.description: Chain rule connects rates of change in nested functions.
